mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of error handling in programming?

options.text: To improve the performance of a program
To handle exceptions and errors
To optimize the code
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: Error handling is primarily used to handle exceptions and errors that may occur during the execution of a program.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a type of error in programming?

options.text: Syntax error
Runtime error
Logical error
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: All of the above options are types of errors in programming.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the try block?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To contain the code that may throw an exception
To optimize the code
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The try block contains the code that may throw an exception.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the catch block?

options.text: To contain the code that may throw an exception
To handle exceptions
To optimize the code
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The catch block contains the code that handles the exception.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the finally block?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To contain the code that may throw an exception
To execute the code regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The finally block contains the code that is executed regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions?

options.text: Checked exceptions are checked at runtime, while unchecked exceptions are checked at compile-time
Checked exceptions are checked at compile-time, while unchecked exceptions are checked at runtime
Checked exceptions are handled by the programmer, while unchecked exceptions are handled by the system
Checked exceptions are thrown by the system, while unchecked exceptions are thrown by the programmer

mcqs.explanation: Checked exceptions are checked at compile-time, while unchecked exceptions are not checked at compile-time.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the throw keyword?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To declare that a method may throw an exception
To explicitly throw an exception
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The throw keyword is used to explicitly throw an exception.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the throws keyword?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To declare that a method may throw an exception
To explicitly throw an exception
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The throws keyword is used to declare that a method may throw an exception.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between error handling and exception handling?

options.text: Error handling is a broader term that includes exception handling
Exception handling is a broader term that includes error handling
Error handling is used to handle syntax errors, while exception handling is used to handle runtime errors
Error handling is used to handle runtime errors, while exception handling is used to handle syntax errors

mcqs.explanation: Error handling is a broader term that includes exception handling, which is a specific mechanism for handling exceptions.

mcqs.question: Why is it important to handle errors in a program?

options.text: To improve the performance of the program
To prevent the program from crashing
To produce unexpected results
To behave in an unintended manner

mcqs.explanation: Handling errors in a program is important to prevent the program from crashing, producing unexpected results, or behaving in an unintended manner.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the error handling mechanism?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To declare that a method may throw an exception
To identify, diagnose, and resolve errors
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of the error handling mechanism is to identify, diagnose, and resolve errors that may occur during the execution of a program.

mcqs.question: What are the benefits of using error handling in a program?

options.text: Improved reliability
Improved robustness
Improved maintainability
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: The benefits of using error handling in a program include improved reliability, robustness, and maintainability.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a syntax error and a runtime error?

options.text: A syntax error occurs at compile-time, while a runtime error occurs at runtime
A syntax error occurs at runtime, while a runtime error occurs at compile-time
A syntax error is a type of runtime error
A runtime error is a type of syntax error

mcqs.explanation: A syntax error occurs at compile-time, while a runtime error occurs at runtime.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the error handling block?

options.text: To handle exceptions
To declare that a method may throw an exception
To identify, diagnose, and resolve errors
To debug the program

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of the error handling block is to identify, diagnose, and resolve errors that may occur during the execution of a program.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between error handling and debugging?

options.text: Error handling is used to identify and resolve errors, while debugging is used to identify and fix bugs
Error handling is used to identify and fix bugs, while debugging is used to identify and resolve errors
Error handling is a type of debugging
Debugging is a type of error handling

mcqs.explanation: Error handling is used to identify and resolve errors, while debugging is used to identify and fix bugs.
